instructions.msg: The Yugabyte datasource authenticates over the PostgreSQL wire protocol with a username / password. Minimal payload: {"url":"localhost:5433","user":"<user>","jsonData":{"database":"<db>"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>"}}. Root-level url and user are read by the backend directly from backend.DataSourceInstanceSettings (pkg/settings.go:25,38); jsonData.database populates Settings.Database via json unmarshal (pkg/settings.go:15,43). Password is stored write-only in secureJsonData.password (pkg/settings.go:39).

URL contract: the backend calls net.SplitHostPort(settings.URL) at pkg/settings.go:25 and returns an error if the URL is missing a port. Configure the URL as 'host:port' (e.g. 'localhost:5433' — Yugabyte YSQL default port is 5433, not the PostgreSQL 5432). Do NOT prefix with a scheme (no 'postgres://' or 'https://'); a scheme will make SplitHostPort fail.

TLS is hardcoded to sslmode='allow' at pkg/settings.go:52 (BuildConnectionString). There is no editor UI or jsonData/secureJsonData field for TLS: no server verification, no client certs, no root-CA pinning. libpq's 'allow' mode first tries a non-TLS connection and falls back to TLS only if the server rejects plaintext, so cluster policy fully determines whether the connection is encrypted. This is a significant divergence from grafana-postgresql-datasource which exposes disable/require/verify-ca/verify-full modes.

secureJsonData.password is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a password is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.password (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.password.

Secure Socks Proxy: the config editor renders a jsonData.enableSecureSocksProxy toggle (ConfigEditor.tsx:83) that is wired into the backend at pkg/driver.go:41-53. This field is intentionally excluded from the registry schema per repo policy; provisioning callers that need it should write jsonData.enableSecureSocksProxy=true directly.

The frontend types (src/types.ts:11) declare YugabyteOptions extends SQLOptions (from @grafana/plugin-ui), so any SQLOptions field (timeInterval, sslmode, sslRootCertFile, maxOpenConns, connMaxLifetime, tlsConfigurationMethod, …) could technically be written to jsonData. The backend (pkg/settings.go) ONLY reads jsonData.database — every other SQLOptions field is silently ignored. Do not model those fields for this plugin; they are dead configuration.
